relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul leaves Athens and comes to Corinth.
Aquila and Priscilla arrive through displacement from Rome.
Shared trade becomes shared life and work.
Every Sabbath, Paul reasons and persuades.
The word presses him to testify Messiah Jesus.
Opposition turns him toward the nations.
The mission moves next door to the synagogue.
Crispus and many Corinthians trust and are baptized.
Night vision commands fearless speech.
The Lord promises presence and much people in the city.
Paul teaches there a year and six months.
Achaia’s judgment seat receives the accusation.
The charge concerns reverence and Law.
Gallio distinguishes civic crime from internal dispute.
Words, names, and Law are returned to their own discernment.
The case is driven away.
Sosthenes is beaten while Gallio remains indifferent.
Paul sails with Priscilla and Aquila after a vow-mark.
Ephesus receives synagogue reasoning.
They ask him to remain longer.
He leaves with openness to return if the Divine wills.
He greets the assembly and returns to Antioch.
He strengthens disciples through Galatia and Phrygia.
Apollos arrives: eloquent and powerful in Scripture.
He knows the Way fervently, though only through John’s baptism.
Priscilla and Aquila explain the Way more accurately.
Grace-trusting disciples are helped by Apollos.
Scripture becomes public proof that Jesus is Messiah.
